Name: FilterManager Page
Purpose:
Provides a dedicated entry point for managing all filters.
Keeps filter administration separated from dashboard execution pages.
Acts as the top-level container route for filter operations.
Input:
Top navigation selection state from the main app.
User click on the FilterManager menu item.
Initial page load context and shared app shell.
High Level Logic:
Checks selected menu key in the main page router.
Mounts the FilterManager component when the key matches.
Delegates all filter CRUD and UI behavior to child logic.
Output:
Filter management screen becomes visible.
Users can browse, create, edit, duplicate, and delete filters.
Page-level ownership of filter workflow is established.

Name: FilterManager Main Container
Purpose:
Central controller for filter lifecycle management.
Coordinates API communication, local UI state, and modal flows.
Maintains list, selection, edit mode, and error/loading states.
Input:
Filter API responses from backend endpoints.
User actions: refresh, create, edit, duplicate, delete, bulk select.
Modal actions from embedded BasicTab editor.
High Level Logic:
Fetches and normalizes filter data into internal state.
Routes actions to save/delete handlers and updates list after changes.
Controls modal open/close and passes data/callbacks to BasicTab.
Output:
Synchronized filter list and actionable UI.
Stable CRUD experience with feedback for loading/errors.
Consistent state transitions for all filter operations.

Name: Filter List Section
Purpose:
Presents all available filters in an operational list format.
Provides quick per-item visibility and status context.
Enables scalable browsing and bulk selection for maintenance tasks.
Input:
Normalized filter array from FilterManager state.
Checkbox selection state and row action callbacks.
Display metadata such as type, active state, and API tag.
High Level Logic:
Iterates through filters and renders one card per record.
Binds edit/duplicate/delete actions to each card control.
Updates selection state for individual and bulk operations.
Output:
Interactive list of filters with actionable controls.
Fast identification of active/inactive and configured filters.
Bulk operations become available when selections exist.

Name: Create/Edit Modal Shell
Purpose:
Creates a focused workspace for filter authoring.
Supports both create and edit flows with shared UI.
Reduces accidental page interactions during configuration.
Input:
Modal visibility flag and editing/new filter payload.
Save and cancel callbacks from parent controller.
Theme wrapper and BasicTab child component.
High Level Logic:
Renders backdrop and centered modal container.
Loads BasicTab with mode-specific props and handlers.
Closes modal on cancel/backdrop and resets transient state.
Output:
Guided form experience for filter definition changes.
Safe create/edit workflow with clear commit points.
Modal lifecycle tied cleanly to parent state.

Name: BasicTab Editor
Purpose:
Captures complete filter configuration in structured tabs.
Separates concerns into basic, styling, events, validation, options, query builder.
Standardizes payload generation for backend persistence.
Input:
Current filter object (new or existing).
Editing mode, parent save/cancel callbacks, and helper setters.
User-entered values across all tabbed sections.
High Level Logic:
Manages active tab and local form state transitions.
Validates field groups and prepares normalized payload parts.
Invokes onSave/onCancel callbacks back to parent controller.
Output:
Comprehensive filter configuration object.
Reduced form complexity through segmented tab workflow.
Reusable editor surface for both create and update use cases.

Name: DashboardManager Page
Purpose:
Provides the main entry point for dashboard administration.
Separates dashboard design/operations from runtime usage pages.
Hosts management features for dashboards, widgets, and data sources.
Input:
Top navigation selection state from the main app.
User click on the DashboardManager menu item.
Shared page shell and routing context.
High Level Logic:
Evaluates selected menu in top-level page logic.
Mounts DashboardManager when the menu key is active.
Passes control to manager component for all domain operations.
Output:
Dashboard management workspace is displayed.
Users can administer structures and linked resources.
Navigation flow remains consistent with other modules.

Name: DashboardManager Main Container
Purpose:
Acts as orchestration hub for dashboard ecosystem.
Combines dashboard, widget, datasource, context menu, and filter assignment workflows.
Maintains coordinated state across tabs and API pipelines.
Input:
API datasets: dashboards, widgets, data sources, filters, mappings.
User actions from tab controls, CRUD buttons, drag/resize, refresh.
Modal submissions and assignment interactions.
High Level Logic:
Initializes and refreshes required datasets.
Switches behavior by active tab and selected dashboard context.
Triggers downstream loaders and update handlers after mutations.
Output:
Unified administrative control panel.
Consistent, stateful operations across all dashboard entities.
Single place to maintain dashboard architecture end-to-end.

Name: Dashboards Tab
Purpose:
Focuses on dashboard-centric management tasks.
Displays available dashboards and the currently selected dashboard workspace.
Enables creation, selection, editing, and deletion at dashboard level.
Input:
Dashboard list, selected dashboard record, widget/layout data.
User actions on dashboard cards and selected panel buttons.
Drag mode and layout-change interactions.
High Level Logic:
Renders dashboard cards for browse/select actions.
Shows selected dashboard details and embedded widget canvas.
Connects CRUD actions to modal handlers and API updates.
Output:
Dashboard-level control with immediate visual context.
Selected dashboard becomes the active design surface.
Widget operations remain scoped to current dashboard.

Name: Widgets Tab
Purpose:
Provides centralized widget inventory management.
Allows inspection and maintenance beyond a single dashboard view.
Supports quick edits/deletes and refresh for widget data.
Input:
Widgets dataset and dashboard lookup metadata.
Row-level actions for edit/delete and add/refresh controls.
Current selection context from manager state.
High Level Logic:
Renders widgets in a management table layout.
Maps widget rows to related dashboard names and positions.
Routes action buttons to widget modal and delete handlers.
Output:
Cross-dashboard widget governance surface.
Faster operational edits without navigating each dashboard.
Clear tabular visibility of widget distribution and types.

Name: Data Sources Tab
Purpose:
Manages all data source definitions used by widgets.
Centralizes endpoint/query/static configuration governance.
Supports lifecycle operations with validation-backed forms.
Input:
Datasource list from API plus modal form state.
User actions: add, edit, delete, refresh.
Source-type specific fields (API/SQL/STATIC) entered by user.
High Level Logic:
Displays data sources in table with actionable controls.
Opens DataSourceModal for create/update with typed validation.
Persists mutations and refreshes list on successful operations.
Output:
Reliable catalog of widget data providers.
Cleaner ownership of connection and payload definitions.
Updated datasource records immediately reflected in manager.

Name: Create Context Menu Tab
Purpose:
Provides configuration space for widget context menus.
Maintains menu trees and assignment links to widgets.
Extends widget interactivity through managed menu actions.
Input:
Context menu records, widget records, assignment mappings.
User-entered menu metadata and assignment selections.
CRUD operations triggered from tab UI.
High Level Logic:
Loads ContextMenuTab component inside manager tab frame.
Executes create/update/delete flows for menus and assignments.
Refreshes local state after each successful mutation.
Output:
Structured context-menu definitions are maintained.
Widgets can be linked to actionable menu entries.
Interactive behavior metadata becomes administratively manageable.

Name: Assign Filters Tab
Purpose:
Controls filter assignment at dashboard and widget scopes.
Allows selective binding of reusable filters to targets.
Improves governance of filter applicability across dashboards.
Input:
Dashboards, widgets, filters, and existing assignment mappings.
Scope selection (dashboard or widget) and selected filter IDs.
Save action to persist mapping payload.
High Level Logic:
Renders AssignFiltersTab with scope-aware selection UI.
Loads assigned filter IDs for the chosen target context.
Saves updated mapping set to backend assignment endpoint.
Output:
Filter-to-target mappings are persisted and visible.
Dashboard/widget behavior can be tuned via assigned filters.
Reusable filter strategy becomes centrally enforceable.
